programme.title: My Best Fiend
programme.desc: In the 1950s, a teenage Werner Herzog was transfixed by a film performance of the young Klaus Kinski. Years later, they would share an apartment where, in an unabated, 48 hour fit of rage, Kinski completely destroyed the bathroom. From this chaos, a violent, love-hate, profoundly creative partnership was born. In 1972, Herzog cast Kinski in Aguirre, Wrath of God. Four more films would follow. In this personal documentary, Herzog traces the often violent ups and downs of their relationship, revisiting the various locations of their films and talking to the people they worked with.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: For the first time in Surviving Mann history, the contestants undergo intense combat training led by none other than Ken Shamrock—"the most dangerous man in the world." His relentless situation drills push this elite group beyond their limits, sharpening their instincts and forging next-level tactical readiness. As a new mission begins to take shape, the pressure mounts—and the team learns that the strongest among them will earn a game-changing reward. But in a world where every edge matters, not everyone is built to keep up. Fatigue sets in, tensions rise, and the difference between teammate and competitor begins to blur. With Shamrock watching closely, only the most disciplined will rise to meet the challenge—and prove they’re worthy of what lies ahead.

programme.title: Death Valley & Mono Lake
programme.desc: Experience the dramatic beauty of Death Valley, from sunrise over the sand dunes to the colorful formations of Zabriske point. Explore the unique limestone formations known as "tufa towers", the amazing bird life, and relaxing serenity of Mono Lake, an inland sea at the base of the Sierra Mountains. Bonus footage of the geothermal "hot creek" in the Sierras. This film is newly extended with awe-inspiring footage of the nesting Ospreys and aerial views of Mono Lake!

programme.title: Locked On NHL
programme.desc:
A rotating cast of local hosts from the Locked On Podcast Network guide you through each day in the National Hockey League with a collection of themed shows. Gil Martin gets started on Mondays by checking in with three of the network's local experts to chat about the biggest stories going on league-wide. Tuesday sees Mike DiStefano and Ross Levitan take stock of the Eastern Conference, followed by a Western Conference check-in on Wednesdays with Brett Holden and Jess Belmosto. Chris Micieli and Adam Denker present league Power Rankings every Thursday. Things wrap up on Fridays as DiBiase and Rachel Donner review the week that was and look ahead to the weekend slate of action in the NHL.
Part of the Locked On Podcast Network.

programme.desc: Railroad detective Matt Clark journeys to the Oklahoma Territory to hunt down Bill Doolin and his notorious gang of outlaws. As the group terrorizes the region with brazen bank and train robberies, Clark must weave through the dangerous frontier to corner the gang and put an end to their violent reign.

programme.title: Stories of the Century S1E22 Little Britches,
programme.desc: Railroad detective Matt Clark pursues Alice "Little Britches" McJunkin, a young woman who has turned to a life of crime in the Wyoming Territory. programme.title: Tombstone Canyon

programme.title: Young Bill Hickok 1940
programme.desc: Roy Rogers saddles up in Young Bill Hickok, an action-packed Western adventure inspired by the early days of the legendary frontier hero. As war clouds gather over a divided nation, Hickok faces treacherous outlaws, secret Confederate plots, and high-stakes gun battles that threaten the peace of the West. With courage, quick draw, and a song on his lips, Rogers brings larger-than-life charm to this thrilling tale of loyalty, justice, and the making of a legend.

programme.title: Apache Rose
